concerns.text: Tasks T004, T014, and T014a reference OpenNeuro dataset 'ds000246' (matching spec FR-001), but the plan.md explicitly mandates 'ds000248'. This creates a critical coverage gap where the tasks do not align with the approved implementation plan's data source, risking execution against the wrong dataset.

Task T035 defines 'is_significant' solely based on 'permutation_p < 0.05'. However, spec SC-001 and US-3 acceptance criteria require the system to verify if the observed statistic falls outside the 95% confidence interval of the null distribution generated by the permutation test. The task description misses the logic to compute/compare against the CI, leaving SC-001 unimplemented.

Task T032 implements 'Single-subject Linear Regression' but the plan.md (Complexity Tracking) and spec US-3 explicitly require a 'Two-Level GLM' (Subject-level + Group-level t-test) to handle non-independence. The tasks lack any implementation steps for the Group-level aggregation and statistical testing, leaving the plan's core methodology unimplemented.

Tasks T039a and T039b (Polish phase) are not tagged to any User Story or Functional Requirement. While they address code quality, they do not map to the specific FR/SC coverage required by the spec. Additionally, T039a extracts memory logic which is already partially covered by T019; the separation is unclear and risks duplication or missing coverage of the memory abort logic in the main pipeline.

Task T005a creates 'data/metadata.yaml' for checksums. The Constitution (Principle V) and Plan (Versioning Discipline) require checksums to be recorded in 'state/projects/...yaml'. The task covers the local file but misses the mandatory state file update, creating a gap in the versioning discipline requirement.
